edges.description: Without BMP1/mTLD C-proteinase activity, the type I procollagen C-propeptide is not cleaved normally, impairing maturation and assembly of collagen fibrils.
edges.source: BMP1/mTLD Loss Abolishes Procollagen C-Proteinase Activity
edges.target: Defective Procollagen Processing and Abnormal Collagen Fibril Assembly

edges.description: The defective collagen scaffold yields an abnormally mineralized bone matrix that, despite high mineral content, is mechanically weak and fracture-prone.
edges.source: Defective Procollagen Processing and Abnormal Collagen Fibril Assembly
edges.target: Abnormally Mineralized Bone Matrix and Skeletal Fragility

nodes.description: The abnormal collagen matrix in BMP1/mTLD deficiency mineralizes aberrantly: histomorphometry shows hyperosteoidosis with delayed onset of mineralization, yet the mineralized matrix becomes hypermineralized (high calcium content). The net result is bone that is paradoxically dense (high areal bone mineral density) but mechanically brittle, producing recurrent fractures, deformity, and short stature. This high-bone-mass fragility distinguishes OI type XIII from the typically low-density recessive OI types.
nodes.id: Abnormally Mineralized Bone Matrix and Skeletal Fragility

evidence.reference_title: Hyperosteoidosis and hypermineralization in the same bone: bone tissue analyses in a boy with a homozygous BMP1 mutation.
evidence.snippet: It thus appears that disturbed C-propeptide cleavage impairs mineralization in two ways: first, the onset of mineralization is delayed, leading to an increased amount of unmineralized osteoid, and second, once mineralization starts, too much mineral is incorporated into the bone matrix, resulting in hypermineralization.

evidence.reference_title: Attenuated BMP1 function compromises osteogenesis, leading to bone fragility in humans and zebrafish.
evidence.snippet: Bmp1a is critically required for mature-collagen generation, downstream of osteoblast maturation, in bone.

nodes.description: BMP1 encodes the secreted astacin metalloprotease bone morphogenetic protein 1 and its longer isoform mammalian Tolloid (mTLD), which together constitute the principal procollagen C-terminal-propeptide (PICP) endopeptidase for type I procollagen. Biallelic loss-of-function BMP1 variants (e.g., the protease-domain p.Phe249Leu missense and signal-peptide variants) destroy or attenuate this proteolytic activity, so excess mutant BMP1/mTLD fails to enhance cleavage of the C-terminal propeptide of proalpha1(I). This is a proteolytic (enzymatic) lesion, mechanistically distinct from the chaperone and prolyl-3-hydroxylation defects of other recessive OI types.
nodes.id: BMP1/mTLD Loss Abolishes Procollagen C-Proteinase Activity

evidence.reference_title: Identification of a mutation causing deficient BMP1/mTLD proteolytic activity in autosomal recessive osteogenesis imperfecta.
evidence.snippet: proalpha1(I) cleavage was not enhanced by an excess of the mutant protein, proving that the Phe249Leu mutation leads to a BMP1/mTLD protein with deficient PICP proteolytic activity.

evidence.reference_title: Attenuated BMP1 function compromises osteogenesis, leading to bone fragility in humans and zebrafish.
evidence.snippet: we found a homozygous causative BMP1 mutation, c.34G>C, in a consanguineous family affected by increased bone mineral density and multiple recurrent fractures.

nodes.description: Deficient BMP1/mTLD activity causes delayed cleavage of the type I procollagen C-propeptide and also hampers processing of prodecorin, a small leucine-rich proteoglycan that regulates collagen fibrillogenesis. The combined defect impairs assembly of mature heterotypic type I/V collagen fibrils in the extracellular matrix, yielding a structurally abnormal bone collagen scaffold.
nodes.id: Defective Procollagen Processing and Abnormal Collagen Fibril Assembly

evidence.reference_title: Defective Proteolytic Processing of Fibrillar Procollagens and Prodecorin Due to Biallelic BMP1 Mutations Results in a Severe, Progressive Form of Osteogenesis Imperfecta.
evidence.snippet: BMP1/mTLD-deficiency in humans not only results in delayed cleavage of the type I procollagen C-propeptide but also hampers the processing of the small leucine-rich proteoglycan prodecorin, a regulator of collagen fibrillogenesis.

evidence.reference_title: Defective Proteolytic Processing of Fibrillar Procollagens and Prodecorin Due to Biallelic BMP1 Mutations Results in a Severe, Progressive Form of Osteogenesis Imperfecta.
evidence.snippet: transmission electron microscopy of the dermis show impaired assembly of heterotypic type I/V collagen fibrils in the ECM.
